skill.name: frontend-dev-guidelines
skill.description: Frontend development guidelines for Next.js 15 applications using React 19, TypeScript, Shadcn/ui, Tailwind CSS, Server Components, and App Router patterns.
skill.summary: Build modern Next.js interfaces with clear component, routing, styling, and performance guidelines.
skill.author: 0Chan-smc

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many command-execution and blocker patterns, but review found they are Markdown inline code, TypeScript examples, headings, or ordinary text in SKILL.md. The only real behavior-related pattern is a documentation example using fetch with api.example.com, which is not executed by the skill. No prompt injection, credential access, malware behavior, or data exfiltration intent was found.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backticks Misidentified as Command Execution
low_findings.description: The external command detections are false positives. The cited content uses Markdown inline code and TypeScript code fences for documentation, not Ruby backtick execution or shell commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged syntax is inside Markdown documentation and TypeScript examples. I found no executable wrapper that would run these snippets as shell commands.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Detections Are Text False Positives
low_findings.description: The high blocker detections do not identify cryptographic code. The cited lines are frontmatter text, section headings, or metadata examples with description strings.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found no md5, sha1, crypto API, hashing routine, or credential handling on the cited lines. The static signal is not supported by context.

low_findings.title: Documentation Example Contains Placeholder Fetch Call
low_findings.description: The skill includes a Next.js data-fetching example that calls api.example.com. This is documentation-only and does not create autonomous network behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The fetch call is a visible placeholder inside a TypeScript example. It would only make a request if a user copied and ran the example in their own app.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Detections Are Component Logic False Positives
low_findings.description: The reconnaissance detections point to React rendering, route parameter handling, and error-boundary type definitions. They do not query host system information.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines operate on example props, route params, or TypeScript types. There is no filesystem, OS, environment, or process inspection.

content.user_title: Build Next.js Frontends with Modern Patterns
content.value_statement: Modern Next.js projects need consistent choices for components, routing, data fetching, and styling. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code practical frontend guidelines for React 19 applications.
actual_capabilities: Guides Server Component and Client Component decisions for Next.js apps.
Shows App Router file structures for pages, layouts, loading states, and errors.
Provides TypeScript component patterns with explicit props and reusable imports.
Explains Tailwind CSS and Shadcn/ui usage with the cn utility.
Covers server-side data fetching, Server Actions, metadata, and route examples.
Lists performance practices for images, fonts, lazy loading, and Suspense.
limitations: It is documentation only and does not run code or inspect a project automatically.
Examples assume a Next.js 15, React 19, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, and Shadcn/ui stack.
It does not replace framework documentation for version-specific API changes.
It does not provide complete accessibility or security review coverage.

use_cases.title: Create a Feature Page
use_cases.description: Use the routing, loading, error, and component structure guidance when adding a new App Router page.

use_cases.title: Standardize Component Patterns
use_cases.description: Apply the Server Component, Client Component, TypeScript, and Shadcn/ui rules across a team codebase.

use_cases.title: Review Frontend Architecture
use_cases.description: Check whether file organization, imports, data fetching, and performance choices match modern Next.js practices.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Simple Component
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the frontend-dev-guidelines skill to create a typed Server Component for a reusable card in my Next.js app.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Route Page
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the frontend-dev-guidelines skill to design a Next.js App Router page with loading and error states.

prompt_templates.title: Refactor Client Logic
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the frontend-dev-guidelines skill to review this component and separate Server Component logic from Client Component logic.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Frontend Structure
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the frontend-dev-guidelines skill to audit my feature folder structure, imports, data fetching, styling, and performance choices.

output_examples.input: Create a typed post list component for a Next.js page.
output_examples.output: A Server Component plan with explicit props, Shadcn/ui card usage, import aliases, and a default data-rendering structure.

output_examples.input: Review whether this form should be a Client Component.
output_examples.output: A boundary recommendation that keeps static rendering on the server and moves hooks, form state, and event handling to the client.

output_examples.input: Plan a new posts route with loading and error states.
output_examples.output: A file layout for the route, guidance for metadata, and separate responsibilities for page, loading, error, and feature components.

best_practices: Default to Server Components and add client boundaries only when interactivity or browser APIs require them.
Use TypeScript prop interfaces, import aliases, and Shadcn/ui components consistently.
Add loading, error, metadata, and performance considerations when creating new routes.
anti_patterns: Do not add use client to an entire page when only one small child needs interactivity.
Do not fetch client-side data with effects when a Server Component can fetch it directly.
Do not place feature-specific components in shared folders before they are truly reusable.

faq.question: Does this skill generate complete applications?
faq.answer: No. It provides frontend guidelines and examples that an AI assistant can apply during implementation.

faq.question: Which stack does it target?
faq.answer: It targets Next.js 15, React 19, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, Shadcn/ui, and the App Router.

faq.question: Does it require network access?
faq.answer: No. The skill is documentation. It includes one placeholder fetch example for data-fetching guidance.

faq.question: Can it help with Server Components?
faq.answer: Yes. It explains when to keep components on the server and when to add client boundaries.

faq.question: Can teams use it as a code review checklist?
faq.answer: Yes. The checklists cover imports, file structure, routing, styling, TypeScript, and performance.

faq.question: Does it include backend API implementation?
faq.answer: No. It focuses on frontend structure and mentions backend consumption only where frontend examples need data.
